text: [Music]
how to order drinks like a bartender if
you get looks of exasperation whenever
you stammer out a drink order take this
crash course in barp you will need a
grasp of liquor and cocktail terminology
step one learn the correct terms to
specify your drink's temperature
ordering it neat means the liquor will
be served right out of the bottle at
room temperature ask for it on the rocks
and it will be poured over ice order it
straight up and the liquor will come out
at whatever temperature is customary
with no ice step two to get two shots of
alcohol in your drink as opposed to the
standard one shot pour ask the bartender
to make it a double step three want a
glass of water or a beer with your drink
request the shot with a water back or
beer back step four when ordering a
mixed drink always name the liquor first
as in a scotch and soda please step five
if you want a specific brand of liquor
say so as in a ketle one and tonic this
is known as a call drink if you don't
name a brand you'll get a well drink
made with a bars default liquor most
often a cheaper bottle naming an
expensive brand of liquor makes your
drink premium or top shelf and you'll
pay a steeper price step six if you want
just a bit of a mixer ask for a splash
as in Scotch with a splash of soda want
more juice or soda in your drink put
tall in front of your drink order the
drink will have the same amount of booze
but will have more mixer because it will
be served in a taller glass step seven
if you'd like a piece of lemon or lime
peel in the drink order it with a Twist
step eight leave a tip a generous one
makes up for any boneheaded ordering did
you know nearly 50% of bartenders pulled
said the customers order fewer but
stronger drinks during a recession
[Music]
